title: 帷幕
summary:
Captain Hastings arrives at a large house via the 5:40 train. He is greeted by Mrs. Lettrell, who mentions that Dr. Franklin rents an underground laboratory on the property for experimental animals, and by her husband, Colonel Lettrell. During their arrival, they encounter Mr. Norton, a bird watcher, and Sir William Boyd-Carters, a tanned, decisive former provincial head in India and skilled hunter. Sir William, who is currently angry with contractors and architects, expresses his familiarity with Hastings through Poirot and makes a comment about Hastings' daughter, Judith.

Lestrade leads Hastings to his room, which has been updated with modern fixtures and partitioned spaces, though Hastings finds the cheap modern décor disappointing. After being interrupted by a sharp cry from Mrs. Lettrell calling for the Colonel, Hastings proceeds to visit Poirot.

Hastings finds Poirot significantly changed by age and arthritis; the detective is now thin, shriveled, and confined to a wheelchair. Poirot has been using hair dye to maintain a black appearance, which Hastings finds comedically out of place. Despite his physical decline, Poirot maintains a sharp mind and a warm affection for Hastings. Poirot complains about the poor quality of the food, the lack of seasoning, and the inadequate modern amenities at the establishment, such as the lukewarm water and thin towels.
